questions.question: Which field of study did W. Somerset Maugham pursue professionally before achieving literary fame?
questions.explanation: Maugham trained as a physician and successfully completed his studies, though he abandoned the practice after his first novel's success.

questions.question: What literary device is most central to the structural organization of 'The Man With the Scar'?
questions.explanation: The story employs a 'story-within-a-story' structure, where a narrator recounts a tale told to him by an acquaintance.

questions.question: How does the narrator characterize the physical appearance of the man with the scar at the Palace Hotel?
options: A juxtaposition of joviality and violent history
An elderly man whose features have been erased by time
A meticulously clean but impoverished gentleman
An intimidating and gaunt military figure
questions.correct: A juxtaposition of joviality and violent history
questions.explanation: The narrator notes the oddity of a 'terrible wound' appearing on a 'round, fat and good-humoured face.'

questions.question: In the account of the night preceding the execution, what serves as a testament to the rebel's composure?
options: Writing a series of farewell letters to his troops
His refusal to accept a final meal
Engaging in a high-stakes card game using matches
questions.correct: Engaging in a high-stakes card game using matches
questions.explanation: The prisoners played poker with matches for chips, highlighting their attempt to maintain normalcy in the face of death.

questions.question: The execution of the four subordinates is described using imagery that evokes:
options: The fluidity and grace of a choreographed dance
The solemnity and stillness of a religious ritual
The chaotic and loud environment of a battlefield
The mechanical and artificial nature of a puppet show
questions.correct: The mechanical and artificial nature of a puppet show
questions.explanation: Maugham uses the simile 'as though they were puppets in a toy theatre' to describe their unnatural fall.

questions.question: What is the primary motivation cited by the rebel for the sudden act of violence against his wife?
options: A desire to spare her from the pain of his death
questions.explanation: When asked why he did it, the rebel simply replies, 'I loved her,' presenting the murder as a paradoxical act of love.

questions.question: The General's decision to pardon the rebel is predicated on which concept?
options: Legal loopholes found in the court-martial documents
A sense of shared military brotherhood and honor
Fear of a renewed uprising if the rebel became a martyr
Sympathy for the man's sudden widower status
questions.correct: A sense of shared military brotherhood and honor
questions.explanation: The General states he honors the rebel 'as one brave man must honour another,' viewing the killing as a 'noble gesture.'

questions.question: The ultimate revelation regarding the origin of the scar serves to:
options: Undermine the gravity and 'high-flown' nature of the previous story
Clarify the specific battle in which the rebel was injured
Suggest that the friend's entire narrative was a fabrication
Validate the narrator's initial heroic assumptions
questions.correct: Undermine the gravity and 'high-flown' nature of the previous story
questions.explanation: The 'burst bottle of ginger ale' creates an anticlimactic irony that punctures the romanticism of the rebel's past.

questions.question: How does the narrator describe the prose and style of the Guatemalan friend who tells the story?
options: Highly sophisticated and grammatically perfect English
Somewhat hyperbolic and elevated in tone
Brief, curt, and focused only on the facts
Clinical and devoid of any emotional resonance
questions.correct: Somewhat hyperbolic and elevated in tone
questions.explanation: The narrator characterizes the friend's speech as 'rather high-flown language,' which he feels suits the dramatic tale.

questions.question: According to the friend, what was the primary reason the rebel's revolution failed?
options: The unexpected arrival of government reinforcements
The exhaustion and desertion of his troops
The depletion of their supply of munitions
A tactical error by the rebel's second-in-command
questions.correct: The depletion of their supply of munitions
questions.explanation: The friend notes that 'if his ammunition hadn't given out,' the rebel would likely be the minister of war.

questions.question: Based on the text's analysis, what does the 'Palace Hotel' setting represent in the frame of the story?
options: The height of Central American luxury and exclusivity
A refuge for those fleeing from the law in Nicaragua
A site of ongoing political intrigue and espionage
A crossroads where extraordinary histories meet mundane reality
questions.correct: A crossroads where extraordinary histories meet mundane reality
questions.explanation: It is the place where the narrator witnesses the 'shabby' reality of a man with a supposedly 'heroic' past.

questions.question: What is the narrator's reaction to the man's refusal to buy lottery tickets?
options: He observes the man's consistent, unbothered politeness
He feels insulted by the man's persistence
He is curious about the man's financial desperation
He believes the man is a nuisance to the hotel guests
questions.correct: He observes the man's consistent, unbothered politeness
questions.explanation: The narrator notes that the man passes on with the 'same smile' when no notice is taken of him.

questions.question: Maugham's 'ironic detachment' is best exemplified in this story by:
options: The anticlimactic explanation of the central visual mystery
The narrator's refusal to buy a lottery ticket
The friend's kind treatment of the ruffian general
The graphic description of the wife's death
questions.correct: The anticlimactic explanation of the central visual mystery
questions.explanation: By resolving the 'terrible' scar with a burst soda bottle, Maugham detachedly mocks the reader's expectation for a tragic origin.

questions.question: How many men, in total, were sentenced to face the firing squad alongside the general?
questions.explanation: The text states there were 'five of them altogether,' implying the general had four companions in the cell.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the wife's appearance as she entered the courtyard?
options: A ragged woman exhausted from long travel
An older woman dressed in colorful traditional attire
A defiant rebel leader shouting for her husband's release
A figure of pale beauty gripped by intense distress
questions.correct: A figure of pale beauty gripped by intense distress
questions.explanation: She is described with a 'dead white' face, 'enormous eyes,' and 'agony on her beautiful face.'

questions.question: What did the rebel do immediately after the first time he kissed his wife in the courtyard?
options: He handed her a lottery ticket for her future
He whispered a secret message in her ear
He fatally wounded her with a concealed blade
He pleaded with the General to spare her life
questions.correct: He fatally wounded her with a concealed blade
questions.explanation: He drew a knife from his shirt and 'stabbed her in the neck' during their embrace.

questions.question: The narrator's final line, 'I never liked it,' refers to:
questions.explanation: After learning it caused the horrific scar, the narrator dryly remarks on his dislike for the beverage.

questions.question: Which novel by Maugham is mentioned as being among his most famous works?
questions.explanation: This 1919 novel is listed alongside his most significant and widely read literary contributions.

questions.question: What is the implied state of the man with the scar during his daily visits to the hotel?
options: Alert and scouting for a new rebellion
questions.explanation: The narrator observes, 'I think he was for the most part a little drunk.'

questions.question: Why did the rebel need to ask for five minutes before his execution?
options: To wait for his ammunition to be delivered
To make a final political speech to the crowd
To finish the poker game with his staff
To say goodbye to his wife waiting at the gate
questions.correct: To say goodbye to his wife waiting at the gate
questions.explanation: He specifically requested to see her after the General asked if the condemned men had any final wishes.

questions.question: In the context of the story, what does the term 'anticlimactic irony' refer to?
options: The hero becoming a common criminal
The rebel surviving while his friends died
The General's inability to carry out the death sentence
The triviality of the cause of a seemingly significant injury
questions.correct: The triviality of the cause of a seemingly significant injury
questions.explanation: The 'anticlimax' occurs when the grand, dramatic expectations for the scar's origin are punctured by a mundane accident.

questions.question: What is the narrator's nationality, according to the biographical information provided?
questions.explanation: Maugham was a British novelist and playwright who often wrote from a British perspective.

questions.question: The friend describes the rebel as a 'ruffian' and a 'bandit.' Why then does he give him money?
options: He is being blackmailed by the former general
He wants to buy all the lottery tickets to win
He believes the man is 'not a bad fellow'
He is a secret supporter of the Nicaraguan rebellion
questions.correct: He believes the man is 'not a bad fellow'
questions.explanation: Despite the man's criminal past, the friend holds a degree of sympathy for his current situation.

questions.question: What does the rebel do after the General tells him he is free to go to the frontier?
options: He thanks the General for his mercy
He attempts to attack the General in a final act of rebellion
He marches to the waiting car in silence
He weeps over the body of his wife
questions.correct: He marches to the waiting car in silence
questions.explanation: He maintains his dignity and silence as he is escorted away between two soldiers.

questions.question: What is the tone of the friend's narrative regarding the events in the courtyard?
options: Critical and condemnatory of the violence
questions.explanation: The description of the wife's beauty, the passion of the kiss, and the 'noble gesture' reflects a romanticized perspective.

questions.question: The description of the soldiers 'giving a gasp of surprise' at the wife's beauty suggests:
options: The universal impact of her extreme distress and grace
They were unaccustomed to seeing women in the prison
They recognized her as a famous Nicaraguan actress
A tactical distraction planned by the rebels
questions.correct: The universal impact of her extreme distress and grace
questions.explanation: Even the 'indifferent' soldiers, hardened by their duty, are momentarily moved by her appearance.

questions.question: Who is the writer of the story 'The Man with the Scar'?
Where does the story 'The Man with the Scar' take place?
What is the occupation of the man with the scar?
Why did the man with the scar come into the Palace Hotel every day?
How did the man get his scar?
options: Due to a bottle that burst when it was opening
Caused by a fragment of a shell
questions.correct: Due to a bottle that burst when it was opening

questions.question: What distinctive feature does the man with the scar have?
How is the man with the scar's physical appearance described?
What type of attire does the man consistently wear?
options: A shabby grey suit, khaki shirt, and a sombrero
questions.correct: A shabby grey suit, khaki shirt, and a sombrero

questions.question: How does the man interact with customers at the Palace Hotel?
options: He engages them with a smile and mentions his numbers
He sits quietly at the bar
questions.correct: He engages them with a smile and mentions his numbers

questions.question: What can be inferred about the man's lifestyle?
options: He struggles financially and often appears under the influence of alcohol
He is well-respected in the community
questions.correct: He struggles financially and often appears under the influence of alcohol

questions.question: What is the overall tone of the narrator's description of the man?
What does the man's rolling walk suggest?
options: He is accustomed to walking long distances
questions.correct: He is accustomed to walking long distances

questions.question: What is the significance of his shabby clothing?
options: It suggests his poor financial situation
It shows his love for fashion
questions.correct: It suggests his poor financial situation

questions.question: How does the narrator perceive the man's interactions?
What does the phrase 'trifle the worse for liquor' imply?
What time of day does the man visit the hotel?
Where does most of the story take place?
Where is the narrator standing when he meets the man?
How does the narrator react to the lottery offer?
How does the narrator's companion respond to the man?
What language does the narrator use to greet the general?
How does the general describe his business?
options: Not so bad, but could be worse
questions.correct: Not so bad, but could be worse

questions.question: What does the general order at the bar?
What does the narrator observe about the general?
options: He has a terrific scar on his face
questions.correct: He has a terrific scar on his face

questions.question: What is the narrator's opinion about the scar?
options: It doesn't add to his beauty
questions.correct: It doesn't add to his beauty

questions.question: From which country is the general originally?
How is the general's character described?
options: A ruffian and a bandit, but not a bad fellow
questions.correct: A ruffian and a bandit, but not a bad fellow

questions.question: What would the general have achieved if his ammunition hadn't run out?
What happened after the general was captured?
options: He was tried by court-martial and sentenced to death
questions.correct: He was tried by court-martial and sentenced to death

questions.question: What did the prisoners do in jail the night before the execution?
What does 'summary in those countries' suggest?
How many men were lined up for execution?
Why was the execution delayed?
options: The firing squad was still being assembled
They were awaiting the general commanding the troops
There was a shortage of ammunition
They were waiting for official witnesses
questions.correct: They were awaiting the general commanding the troops

questions.question: Who is the general mentioned in the story?
What did the condemned man request before his execution?
options: To say goodbye to his wife
questions.correct: To say goodbye to his wife

questions.question: How many prisoners were court-martialed?
Where did the narrator first meet the man with the scar?
options: Bar of the Palace Hotel, Guatemala City
questions.correct: Bar of the Palace Hotel, Guatemala City

questions.question: Which hotel did the man visit daily?
What did the man do the night before his execution?
What was the condemned man's last wish?
options: To say goodbye to his wife
questions.correct: To say goodbye to his wife

questions.question: From which country was the man with the scar living in exile?
How did the man with the scar earn a living?
